mechanism_narrative: A low federal funds rate near zero reduces the discount rate on future corporate cash flows and increases the relative attractiveness of equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward rank correlation between FEDFUNDS and 20-day returns is positive and significant (p < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8 percent signals substantial labor market slack that weakens consumer spending and compresses corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of months when UNRATE exceeds 8 percent.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of elevated consumer prices and a near-zero federal funds rate creates a negative real interest rate environment that suppresses real returns on safe assets and drives capital reallocation into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond what either series predicts independently.
failure_condition: An F-test shows the interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS adds no incremental explanatory power for 20-day returns (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In an early recovery period characterized by high unemployment and zero interest rates, month-over-month increases in retail sales signal reviving consumer demand and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: early recovery with high unemployment and zero rates
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS changes and 5-day returns falls below 0.04 during early recovery periods over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production index signal contracting manufacturing output and broader supply-side deterioration, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances following two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding labor income and household spending capacity, which increases expected corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between PAYEMS and 60-day forward returns is negative and statistically significant (p < 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 600 thousand units indicate a depressed residential construction sector and weak household formation, which reduces demand for building materials and durable goods, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of observations when HOUST is below 600.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP levels reflect stronger aggregate demand that translates into higher corporate sales and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDPC1 level and 60-day forward returns is not positive and significant (p > 0.05) over any 5-year window.
